children.name: Ifrita
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Blue-capped Ifrita, a small, active passerine endemic to the montane forests of New Guinea. It is a remarkable bird in many ways: it is one of the few known passerines to possess a powerful neurotoxin, batrachotoxin, in its skin and feathers, and its plumage combines vivid blue, yellow, and black in a distinctive pattern. It was long placed with the whistlers.
